Title: Green leaf volatiles and jasmonic acid enhance susceptibility to anthracnose diseases caused by Colletotrichum graminicola in maize

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Green leaf volatile and jasmonic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: 13-lipoxygenase branch feeding both HPL/GLV and AOS/JA pathways
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: 13S-hydroperoxy-octadecatrienoic acid (13S-HPOTE); 12-OPDA-related precursor pool; green leaf volatile precursors
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxygenates C18 unsaturated fatty acids to 13S-hydroperoxides that can be channeled into GLV synthesis via HPL or JA synthesis via the AOS pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key upstream control point for GLV production and major producer of 12-OPDA in this study
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool and induces JA through GLV signaling; also contributes to precursor formation for JA
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: In this pathosystem, GLVs and JA enhance susceptibility to anthracnose caused by C. graminicola, partly through suppression of SA-related defenses
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of LOX10 reduces GLV and jasmonate accumulation, elevates SA-associated resistance, and confers resistance to anthracnose leaf blight and stalk rot; exogenous GLVs restore JA accumulation and susceptibility
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: knockout mutants (lox10-2, lox10-3) in B73 and W438; disease lesion phenotyping; LC-MS/MS hormone profiling; exogenous GLV rescue; LOX10-YFP subcellular localization
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Chloroplasts, especially bundle sheath chloroplasts; also mesophyll and guard cell chloroplasts
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native promoter for LOX10-YFP localization line
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Colletotrichum graminicola infection; exogenous GLV pretreatment; GLV treatment during necrotrophy; field-like outdoor growth for stalk assays; humidity chamber during leaf inoculation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS/MS metabolite/hormone profiling; no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: LOX10/GLV/JA activity promotes pathogen susceptibility in this hemibiotrophic interaction; likely intersects with herbivory/wound responses
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: LOX10 is a candidate target for reducing anthracnose susceptibility, but editing may compromise GLV- and JA-mediated wound/herbivore defenses and other oxylipin functions
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GLVs restored susceptibility of lox10 mutants before and after infection; during biotrophy this required JA, whereas during necrotrophy GLVs acted through an unknown JA-independent mechanism. LOX10 appears to be a major producer of 12-OPDA in stalks and leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LOX10 promotes susceptibility of maize leaves and stalks to C. graminicola. 2. lox10 mutants show reduced 12-OPDA, JA, and JA-Ile and increased SA-associated resistance, especially early in infection. 3. Exogenous GLVs fully restore JA/JA-Ile accumulation and susceptibility in lox10 mutants, showing that LOX10-derived GLVs induce JA-mediated susceptibility. 4. LOX10 localizes to chloroplasts, supporting a direct role in oxylipin biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces 12-OPDA in the JA biosynthetic pathway to support formation of jasmonic acid and JA-Ile
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: required redundantly with OPR8 for JA production in maize; key control point established genetically
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor leading to jasmonic acid
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: In this study, JA promotes susceptibility to C. graminicola during early biotrophic infection
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Single opr7 mutants retain wild-type JA and susceptibility due to redundancy with OPR8; loss of OPR7 together with OPR8 abolishes early JA accumulation and increases resistance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: single mutant and double mutant analysis; lesion phenotyping; LC-MS/MS hormone profiling; GLV and MeJA rescue experiments using opr7-5 opr8-2 double mutants
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: C. graminicola infection; exogenous GLVs; MeJA; MeSA
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS/MS metabolite/hormone profiling; no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reducing OPR7/OPR8-dependent JA may improve resistance to this hemibiotroph but could impair jasmonate-dependent wound and herbivore responses
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating OPR7 together with OPR8 could reduce anthracnose susceptibility, but redundancy means single-gene alteration is insufficient and JA-related defense tradeoffs are likely
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: OPR7 alone was not sufficient to change phenotype because OPR8 compensates. The paper suggests GLVs may induce OPR7/OPR8 expression/activity downstream of 12-OPDA.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OPR7 functions redundantly with OPR8 in JA biosynthesis. 2. Single opr7 mutants remain susceptible with wild-type JA levels. 3. Combined loss of OPR7 and OPR8 strongly reduces early JA/JA-Ile, elevates resistance, and blocks GLV-mediated rescue.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces 12-OPDA in the JA biosynthetic pathway to support formation of jasmonic acid and JA-Ile
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: required redundantly with OPR7 for JA production in maize; key control point established genetically
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor leading to jasmonic acid
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: In this study, JA promotes susceptibility to C. graminicola during early biotrophic infection
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Single opr8 mutants retain wild-type JA and susceptibility due to redundancy with OPR7; loss of OPR8 together with OPR7 abolishes early JA accumulation and increases resistance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: single mutant and double mutant analysis; lesion phenotyping; LC-MS/MS hormone profiling; GLV and MeJA rescue experiments using opr7-5 opr8-2 double mutants
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: C. graminicola infection; exogenous GLVs; MeJA; MeSA
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS/MS metabolite/hormone profiling; no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reducing OPR7/OPR8-dependent JA may improve resistance to this hemibiotroph but could impair jasmonate-dependent wound and herbivore responses
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating OPR8 together with OPR7 could reduce anthracnose susceptibility, but redundancy means single-gene alteration is insufficient and JA-related defense tradeoffs are likely
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: OPR8 alone was not sufficient to change phenotype because OPR7 compensates. Late JA accumulation in the double mutant suggests possible alternative JA source/pathway at necrotrophic stages.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OPR8 functions redundantly with OPR7 in JA biosynthesis. 2. Single opr8 mutants remain susceptible with wild-type JA levels. 3. Combined loss of OPR7 and OPR8 strongly reduces early JA/JA-Ile, elevates resistance, and prevents GLV-mediated rescue of susceptibility.
